```python
import pytest
from collections import OrderedDict  # For reference, but not directly used in tests

# The function under test (pasted here for self-contained test file)
def lru_cache(capacity: int):
    cache = OrderedDict()
    def get(key):
        if key in cache:
            cache.move_to_end(key)
            return cache[key]
        return -1
    def put(key, value):
        if key in cache:
            cache.move_to_end(key)
        cache[key] = value
        if len(cache) > capacity:
            cache.popitem(last=False)
    return get, put


class TestLRUCache:
    """Unit tests for the LRU Cache implementation."""

    @pytest.fixture
    def create_cache(self, capacity):
        """Fixture to create an LRU cache with given capacity."""
        return lru_cache(capacity)

    def test_happy_path_basic_operations(self, create_cache):
        """Happy path: Basic put and get operations with capacity > 1."""
        get, put = create_cache(2)

        # Put two items
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)

        # Get existing items (should move to end, but order doesn't affect correctness here)
        assert get(1) == 10
        assert get(2) == 20

        # Update existing key
        put(1, 15)
        assert get(1) == 15  # Updated value
        assert get(2) == 20  # Still there

    def test_happy_path_eviction(self, create_cache):
        """Happy path: Eviction when capacity is exceeded."""
        get, put = create_cache(2)

        # Fill cache
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        put(3, 30)  # Should evict key 1 (LRU)

        assert get(1) == -1  # Evicted
        assert get(2) == 20
        assert get(3) == 30

        # Access 2 (moves to MRU), then put 4 (evicts 3, since 2 is now MRU)
        get(2)
        put(4, 40)
        assert get(3) == -1  # Evicted
        assert get(2) == 20
        assert get(4) == 40

    def test_edge_case_capacity_zero(self, create_cache):
        """Edge case: Capacity 0 - cache should always be empty."""
        get, put = create_cache(0)

        # Put should add and immediately evict
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == -1

        put(2, 20)
        assert get(2) == -1
        assert get(1) == -1

        # Multiple puts
        put(1, 15)
        put(3, 30)
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(3) == -1

    def test_edge_case_capacity_one(self, create_cache):
        """Edge case: Capacity 1 - only one item at a time."""
        get, put = create_cache(1)

        # Put first item
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == 10

        # Put second item - evicts first
        put(2, 20)
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 20

        # Update existing
        put(2, 25)
        assert get(2) == 25

        # Get non-existent
        assert get(3) == -1

    def test_edge_case_get_non_existent(self, create_cache):
        """Edge case: Get non-existent key returns -1."""
        get, put = create_cache(3)

        # Empty cache
        assert get(1) == -1

        # After some puts
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        assert get(3) == -1  # Non-existent

    def test_edge_case_duplicate_puts(self, create_cache):
        """Edge case: Multiple puts for the same key update value and move to MRU."""
        get, put = create_cache(2)

        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        put(1, 15)  # Update 1, moves to MRU

        # Now order: 1 (MRU), 2 (LRU)
        put(3, 30)  # Evicts 2
        assert get(1) == 15
        assert get(2) == -1
        assert get
